title: Helmerich & Payne vs. Noble: Which Energy Services Stock Is a Better Buy in 2026?
author: Brendan Coffey
description: The article compares two energy services companies: Helmerich & Payne, a land-based drilling specialist, and Noble Corp., an offshore drilling contractor. While both companies benefit from strong U.S. energy markets, Helmerich & Payne is recommended as the better 2026 investment due to its 67% U.S. revenue exposure, lower valuation multiples (P/E of 23.3x vs. Noble's 40.5x), and better positioning to capitalize on higher oil prices benefiting domestic producers.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Recommended as the better buy for 2026 due to strong U.S. market exposure (67% of sales), lower valuation multiples (P/E 23.3x), solid revenue growth (35.9% YoY), and better positioning to benefit from higher oil prices in the domestic market.

Solid fundamentals with strong cash flow ($953.91M), conservative balance sheet (0.4x debt-to-equity), and stable offshore operations, but higher valuation multiples (P/E 40.5x, P/S 2.2x), lower revenue growth (7.4% YoY), and profit margin compression make it less attractive than Helmerich & Payne for 2026.

title: A PTEN Director Sold $123K in Shares - what he kept adds context
author: Seena Hassouna
description: Patterson-UTI Energy director Jaime Cesar sold 10,000 shares (11.43% of his direct holdings) for approximately $123,000 on May 5, 2026. However, he retained $961,000 worth of stock, suggesting confidence in the company. The article characterizes this as a routine trim rather than a significant exit signal, noting it was a deliberate open-market transaction rather than a pre-scheduled plan sale.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: While the director's sale of 11% of holdings could signal concern, the retention of nearly $1 million in stock suggests continued confidence. The article explicitly states this is a 'trim, not an exit' and characterizes the transaction as 'background noise' for investors, indicating no meaningful negative signal about company fundamentals or outlook.

title: 3 Cheap Mid-Cap Energy Stocks to Own as Oil Prices Surge to $100
author: Jesse Cohen
description: Rising geopolitical tensions in the Middle East have driven crude oil prices near $100 per barrel, creating favorable conditions for energy stocks. The article highlights three undervalued mid-cap energy companies positioned to benefit from higher oil prices and increased exploration activity.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Gulf of Mexico offshore producer with oil-heavy production mix benefits directly from surging oil prices. InvestingPro rates it a Buy with 38.6% upside to fair value, 43.5% EPS growth forecast for 2026, and robust 19.8% free cash flow yield.

Drilling and pressure pumping services provider thrives on increased exploration activity from elevated oil prices. Boasts 61% YTD return, Buy consensus rating, 22.5% fair value upside, and positive post-earnings momentum with Goldman Sachs price target support.

Non-operator with interests in Williston and Permian Basins offers stable, royalty-like revenues from oil-weighted assets. Trading at 9.7 forward P/E (sector discount), 12.7% fair value upside, best Financial Health score in the group, and defensive appeal with steady dividend.

title: SLB, ADNOC Drilling, Patterson-UTI Join Forces To Accelerate UAE's Unconventional Oil Program
author: Lekha Gupta
description: Schlumberger, ADNOC Drilling, and Patterson-UTI have formed a joint venture to accelerate the UAE's unconventional oil and gas program, with a target of completing 144 wells by 2025.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Schlumberger is a key partner in the joint venture, providing drilling, completion services, project management, and digital solutions. The article highlights Schlumberger's leading position in the region and its ability to deploy innovative technologies to develop the UAE's unconventional energy resources.

Patterson-UTI is a partner in the joint venture, holding a 15% stake. The article suggests that the partnership will leverage the companies' expertise and technologies to drive the UAE's unconventional energy program.
The article mentions that investors can gain exposure to Schlumberger through the VanEck Oil Services ETF, indicating that the ETF may benefit from Schlumberger's involvement in the joint venture.

Similar to the VanEck Oil Services ETF, the article suggests that investors can gain exposure to Schlumberger through the iShares U.S. Oil Equipment & Services ETF, which may also benefit from Schlumberger's participation in the joint venture.

title: Patterson-UTI (PTEN) Q2 Earnings Fall Y/Y, Sales Lag Estimates
author: N/A
description: Patterson-UTI Energy reported lower Q2 2024 earnings compared to the prior year due to weaker performance in its Drilling Services segment. The company's Completion Services segment saw strong revenue growth, but missed expectations. Patterson-UTI Energy returned $164 million to shareholders in Q2 and has $819 million remaining in its share repurchase authorization.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company reported lower earnings compared to the prior year, but its Completion Services segment saw strong revenue growth, and it returned significant capital to shareholders.
The company reported lower earnings compared to the prior year, but returned capital to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases.
The company reported earnings in line with expectations and saw strength in its international markets.
The company reported earnings in line with expectations and saw strong financial contributions from its business segments.
